commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Needs Doctor owner review and latest-head real behavior proof; there is no narrow code defect for an automated repair worker to fix.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the diff does not change dependencies, workflows, secrets handling, package resolution, or downloaded code execution paths. (sha=d139d39e884019ce5de521660ba382a0499a1575)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds internal health-check adapter/types and refactors `runDoctorHealthRepairs` so split `detect`/`repair` checks and internal run-style Doctor checks share one repair runner path, with focused lint and repair-flow tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This is a refactor/API-shape PR rather than a bug report; source review plus latest-head Doctor CLI proof are the relevant checks.
commands.review_followup: Needs Doctor owner review and latest-head real behavior proof; there is no narrow code defect for an automated repair worker to fix.

target.title: Doctor: normalize repair checks inside runner
target.body:
## Summary

This PR narrows the first doctor repair migration step to the core doctor runner.

- Adds an internal normalized health-check shape so the repair runner can execute either the existing split `detect()` / `repair()` checks or an internal single-flow `run(ctx)` check.
- Keeps the public plugin SDK health contract unchanged: plugin authors still see `detect()` plus optional `repair()`, and no plugin SDK baseline is regenerated.
- Preserves the old split-check repair sequencing so detected findings are still reported if `repair()` throws.
- Threads dry-run, diff, repair effects, warnings, config updates, and post-repair validation through the normalized runner path.
- Adds focused lint and repair-flow tests covering run-mode checks, split-check adaptation, dry-run/diff preview behavior, repair status mapping, repair failure reporting, and validation after successful repair.

The goal is to make the runner easier to migrate safely without asking every existing doctor check author to split one boolean repair flow immediately. Existing split checks still work through the adapter; internal checks can move to one flow when that is clearer.

## Verification

Completed after the final patch:

- `git diff --check`
- `OPENCLAW_OXLINT_SKIP_PREPARE=1 node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.ts src/flows/health-check-adapter.ts src/flows/health-check-runner-types.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-prod-83753.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-83753-pr1-contract.tsbuildinfo`

Earlier focused proof on this branch after adding the split repair-failure regression test:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts` — 2 files / 14 tests passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --engine codex` found the split repair-failure regression; this patch includes that fix and regression test.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Doctor repair runner now has one internal normalized execution path while preserving existing split-check repair error behavior, lint behavior, repair behavior, dry-run/diff previews, effects, warnings, config updates, and validation behavior.

Real environment tested: WSL2 Ubuntu checkout rebased onto latest `origin/main`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused lint and typecheck commands listed above, plus `git diff --check`.

Evidence after fix: focused lint/typecheck passed; earlier targeted doctor flow tests passed after the repair-failure regression test was added.

Observed result after fix: the public plugin SDK health surface remains unchanged while the core doctor runner can adapt split checks and internal run checks.

What was not tested: final Vitest rerun after the last adapter marker guard; the local WSL Vitest process hung and was killed, so CI should be treated as the final Vitest authority for this pushed head.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: giodl73-repo
commands.repair_reason: Manual review is needed because the PR has a protected maintainer label and the remaining blocker is live Windows/WSL2 proof rather than a mechanical code repair.; Cleared: The diff narrows Windows process termination for node services and does not add dependencies, workflow permissions, secrets handling, package resolution, or other supply-chain surfaces. (sha=9204441152acbc9ace41eff1ed369eed053f3935)
commands.review_summary:
The branch scopes Windows Scheduled Task node lifecycle handling away from Gateway listener port cleanup, adds node-host command-line process matching, and expands schtasks stop/startup fallback regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main lets node service stop/restart parse the node run Gateway connection port and then perform Gateway listener cleanup and wait/force-kill behavior. I did not run a live Windows/WSL2 reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Manual review is needed because the PR has a protected maintainer label and the remaining blocker is live Windows/WSL2 proof rather than a mechanical code repair.

target.title: Doctor: normalize repair checks inside runner
target.body:
## Summary

This PR narrows the first doctor repair migration step to the core doctor runner.

- Adds an internal normalized health-check shape so the repair runner can execute either the existing split `detect()` / `repair()` checks or an internal single-flow `run(ctx)` check.
- Keeps the public plugin SDK health contract unchanged: plugin authors still see `detect()` plus optional `repair()`, and no plugin SDK baseline is regenerated.
- Preserves the old split-check repair sequencing so detected findings are still reported if `repair()` throws.
- Threads dry-run, diff, repair effects, warnings, config updates, and post-repair validation through the normalized runner path.
- Adds focused lint and repair-flow tests covering run-mode checks, split-check adaptation, dry-run/diff preview behavior, repair status mapping, repair failure reporting, and validation after successful repair.

The goal is to make the runner easier to migrate safely without asking every existing doctor check author to split one boolean repair flow immediately. Existing split checks still work through the adapter; internal checks can move to one flow when that is clearer.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
